page: Amazon Web Services c7a.32xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro System. It features 128 dedicated physical cores and 128 vCPUs utilizing the x86_64 AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 256 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. There is no local storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance type, meaning storage requirements must be met via network-attached options. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, with the stress-ng multi-core test and PassMark CPU score placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is also highly rated, with read-only large block bandwidth reaching 565.2 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 294.16 GB/sec. The processor's instruction set extensions contribute to top-tier results in encryption, physics simulations, and software compilation. However, single-threaded performance is relatively lower, with gzip compression and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression scoring in the average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c7a.32xlarge delivers high compute density and memory bandwidth per resource unit, making it suitable for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. The primary tradeoff is the low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2 GB per core and the lack of local NVMe storage. This configuration is optimized for workloads such as high-performance computing, batch processing, distributed analytics, CPU-based machine learning inference, and intensive web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services c7a.32xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 128 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. It is configured with 256 GB of DDR5 memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core, and lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance provides 50 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results show top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth, alongside strong database and cryptographic execution. However, single-threaded performance and multi-threaded decompression fall into the average tier. This resource profile offers high compute density for parallel processing but presents a tradeoff for memory-intensive applications. It is suited for compute-heavy workloads such as software compilation, physics simulations, and CPU-based language model prompt processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.32xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 128 AMD EPYC cores, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.32xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 128 AMD EPYC cores, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring 128 dedicated AMD EPYC cores and DDR5 memory optimized for parallel processing and cryptographic workloads.
bullet_points: 128 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 physical cores running at 3.7 GHz
256 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory (2 GB per core)
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier multi-core CPU, encryption, and memory bandwidth benchmarks
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Optimized for software compilation, physics simulations, and batch processing
